description:
## Deep Antagonistic Audit — HELiX Tier 1 Component Library

**Trigger:** This feature should be executed AFTER all T1 component tickets (T1-01 through T1-20) have been merged to dev.

**Purpose:** A comprehensive, adversarial quality audit of the entire Tier 1 component library. This is the gate between "components exist" and "enterprise-grade, world-class component infrastructure." No component ships to production until this audit passes.

---

### Audit Dimensions

#### 1. TypeScript Strictness Audit
- Zero `any` types across ALL component files, test files, and story files
- Zero non-null assertions (`!`) — must use proper null guards
- Zero `@ts-ignore` or `@ts-expect-error`
- All public APIs have explicit return types
- Generic types used where appropriate (not `unknown` casts)
- Run `npm run type-check` — must be zero errors

#### 2. Design Token Compliance Audit
- ZERO hardcoded colors, spacing, typography, or timing values in ANY `.styles.ts` file
- Every CSS custom property follows the three-tier cascade: `var(--hx-component-prop, var(--hx-semantic-prop))`
- No raw hex values (#xxx), no raw rem/px values as primary values
- All components use consistent token naming conventions
- Cross-component consistency: same semantic tokens used for same purposes
- Create a token coverage matrix: which tokens each component uses

#### 3. Accessibility Deep Dive (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Every component tested with axe-core in ALL states (default, disabled, error, loading, etc.)
- Keyboard navigation verified: Tab order, arrow keys, Enter/Space activation, Escape dismissal
- Screen reader testing: NVDA/VoiceOver announcement verification for all interactive components
- Focus management: visible focus rings, focus trap in modals/dropdowns, focus restoration
- ARIA patterns verified against WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG)
- Color contrast ratios meet 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for large text and UI components
- Form components: label association, error announcement, required indicator, validation messages

#### 4. API Consistency Audit
- Property naming consistency across all components (e.g., `hx-size` everywhere, not mixed)
- Event naming consistency (`hx-*` prefix, `composed: true`, proper detail types)
- Slot naming consistency (same slot names for same concepts across components)
- CSS Part naming consistency
- CEM accuracy: `npm run cem` output matches actual public API for EVERY component
- No undocumented public properties, methods, events, or slots

#### 5. Test Coverage & Quality Audit
- Minimum 80% coverage per component (line, branch, function)
- Tests cover: default render, all property variations, slot projection, event dispatch, keyboard interaction, form submission, error states, disabled states
- No flaky tests (run suite 3x, all must pass)
- Test isolation: no test depends on another test's state
- Shadow DOM queries use proper test utilities (not `querySelector` on light DOM)
- No `setTimeout` hacks — use proper async patterns

#### 6. Storybook Story Completeness
- Every component has stories for ALL variants, sizes, and states
- Controls for every public property
- Interaction tests (play functions) for key behaviors
- Composition stories showing components used together
- Drupal integration stories showing Twig template patterns
- Accessibility addon shows zero violations in every story

#### 7. Performance & Bundle Size Audit
- Each component < 5KB minified + gzipped
- Total library bundle < 50KB
- No unnecessary dependencies between components
- Tree-shaking works: importing one component doesn't pull in others
- Render performance: first paint under 16ms for each component
- No memory leaks (attach/detach cycles)

#### 8. Shadow DOM & Encapsulation Audit
- No style leakage in or out of shadow DOM
- CSS Parts properly expose styling hooks without breaking encapsulation
- Slot projection works correctly for all slot configurations
- `::slotted()` selectors used appropriately (not for deep children)
- Components render correctly when nested inside other shadow DOM components

#### 9. Drupal Integration Readiness
- All components work in Twig templates without JavaScript initialization
- Custom events bubble through shadow DOM (`composed: true`)
- Form components participate in native `<form>` submission via ElementInternals
- Components handle dynamic content injection (Drupal AJAX, BigPipe)
- No dependencies on module bundlers (works via CDN `<script>` tag)

#### 10. Infrastructure & Tooling Audit
- CI pipeline catches all quality issues (lint, format, type-check, test, build)
- CEM generation is automated and accurate
- Storybook builds successfully with all stories
- Package exports are correct (ESM, types, side effects)
- `package.json` exports map is complete and correct
- Vite library mode config has proper entry points per component

---

### Deliverables

1. **Audit Report** — Per-component scorecard across all 10 dimensions
2. **Critical Issues List** — Ordered by severity, with specific file:line references
3. **Remediation Plan** — Board features for each critical issue found
4. **Component Maturity Matrix** — Grade each component A-F across dimensions
5. **Infrastructure Gaps** — What's missing from CI/tooling to prevent future regressions

### Acceptance Criteria
- All 10 audit dimensions executed against every T1 component
- Every finding has a specific file path, line number, and proposed fix
- Critical findings become new board features with proper dependencies
- Final maturity score calculated for the library as a whole
- Report submitted as a structured document in the repo
title: FINAL AUDIT: Deep Antagonistic Review of Tier 1 Component Library
